//axhob_operation
{bust_garrison_wsl}

NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell,
for Windows nodes reached via the WSL distribution `rbtww-main`
(hardcoded in {bujb_jurisdiction}). On Windows the workload identity
inhabits *three namespaces*: a Windows local user (created via
`net.exe user /add` plus profile registration in
`HKLM\...\ProfileList`) provides the SSH authentication boundary that
Windows OpenSSH discovers; the workload user's *own* registered WSL
distribution (populated in their `HKCU\Lxss` via `wsl --import` from
a seed tarball) provides the distribution registration the workload's
session resolves; and a Linux user inside that workload-owned
distribution (created via `useradd`) provides the WSL execution
context entered by the locked-down `command=` directive. The third
namespace is forced by Microsoft's per-user WSL design: distributions
register only in the running user's `HKCU\Lxss`, and admin's
distributions are invisible to workload sessions.

The mechanism is *SSH-as-workload conducted by the privileged
orchestrator*. The orchestrator never runs `wsl` as the workload
user directly. Instead it places a *bare* workload authorized_keys
entry (no `command=`), opens a workload SSH session through that
entry — which constitutes a real Windows logon for
{bujb_workload_user}, with HKCU mounted — and from inside that
session runs `wsl --import` (writing to the workload's HKCU), the
inner-WSL `useradd` + `passwd --lock`, and the workload privkey
placement. The orchestrator then replaces the bare entry with the
locked-down `command=` form and validates the round-trip through
that locked form.

The Windows workload user is the same shape minted by the c branch
({bust_garrison_cygwin}); the workload-owned WSL distribution and
its inner Linux user are layered on top so the SSH session lands as
the named WSL user (via the locked-down `command=` directive's
`wsl.exe --user`) rather than the distribution's default user.

Workload-only: garrison destroys any prior workload state across all
three namespaces via `zbujb_obliterate_workload` in
{bujb_jurisdiction} — the single greppable destroy verb. The
covenant scope spans the Windows SAM entry, the `C:\Users\<user>\`
Windows profile directory (by convention containing the workload's
WSL filesystem root, so purging the profile transitively destroys
the workload's WSL distribution registration's on-disk filesystem
and the HKCU hive that referenced it), the `C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\`
Cygwin home, and the WSL-side Linux user inside admin's `rbtww-main`
(vestige from prior garrison shapes; idempotent on absent state).

Garrison never reads or writes any `sshd_config` and never modifies
admin authorized_keys; admin trust is established beforehand by
{busn_caparison} on the Windows OpenSSH server, while sshd hardening
inside the WSL distribution remains operator-manual.

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
referenced {burn_regime} profile is reachable.

//axhoq_precondition
{busn_caparison} has run for this {burp_investiture} so that
{burp_privileged_user} can authenticate by key alone using
{burp_privileged_key_file} into the Windows OpenSSH host (the admin
entry point); the workload SSH session is then opened from that
foothold using the workload pubkey just placed by garrison.

//axhoq_precondition
{bust_invigilate_windows} passes for this {burp_investiture}: admin
SSH key trust is in place, the operator-managed registry posture is
correct, the Tailscale service is registered and set to Automatic,
powercfg sleep/hibernate are disabled, and the `rbtww-main` WSL
distribution (canonical name hardcoded as `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in
{bujb_jurisdiction}) is registered under the admin user. Garrison
runs invigilate as its first action so a missing or stale admin
posture surfaces with a fact-named diagnostic rather than as an
opaque downstream failure.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Assert admin posture*: invoke `{bust_invigilate_windows}` for this
{burp_investiture}. Failure surfaces a fact-named diagnostic
({bust_handbook_windows} pointer for operator-managed facts;
{bust_caparison_windows} pointer for caparison-managed facts) and
aborts garrison before any destructive workload work.

//axhos_step
. *Open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} using
{burp_privileged_key_file} into the Windows OpenSSH host; key-auth
only. Failure here surfaces a missing or stale admin trust.

//axhos_step
. *Export seed*: export admin's `rbtww-main` distribution to a
workload-readable seed tarball under a path inside the workload's
profile (admin can write there before the workload first logs in,
and the workload's session can read it). Caparison has staged
`rbtww-main` and invigilate has verified its presence as a
precondition; this step takes that presence as given. The tarball
is the sole on-disk artifact carrying admin's `rbtww-main` contents
into the workload's identity scope; garrison removes it on success.

//axhos_step
. *Obliterate workload*: invoke `zbujb_obliterate_workload`, the
letter-agnostic destroy helper. On Windows the helper executes a
single PowerShell session over admin SSH that purges the Windows
SAM entry (`net.exe user /delete`), the Windows profile directory
(`C:\Users\<user>\`, including any prior workload-owned WSL
filesystem under that path), the Cygwin home
(`C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\`), and the WSL-side Linux user inside
admin's `rbtww-main` (vestige from prior garrison shapes;
idempotent on absent state).

//axhos_step
. *Create Windows workload account*: provision {bujb_workload_user}
as a Windows local user via
`net.exe user /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes` (matching the c
branch), then register the resulting SID under
`HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList` so
Windows OpenSSH builds a complete logon session for the workload
on inbound SSH (Win32-OpenSSH issue #1383). No Linux user is
created inside admin's rbtww-main; the workload's Linux user is
minted inside the workload's *own* imported distribution in the
SSH-as-workload step below.

//axhos_step
. *Place bare workload trust*: write the workload pubkey (derived
via `ssh-keygen -y` from {burp_workload_key_file}) to the
Windows-side authorized_keys at
`/mnt/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/authorized_keys` so Windows
OpenSSH discovers it natively. The directory is created with
`mkdir -p` and chmod 700; the file is written and chmod 600. No
POSIX `chown` is performed — `/mnt/c/...` ownership is meaningless
to Windows OpenSSH, which inspects NTFS ACLs; the inherited NTFS
ACL from the parent profile directory is admin-owned and read-only
for non-admins, satisfying StrictModes. The entry is *bare* — no
`command=` directive — because the locked-down `command=` would
route through a WSL distribution that has not yet been imported
under the workload's HKCU. The bare-then-lockdown pattern is
structural to this ceremony; the workload pubkey is derived from
{burp_workload_key_file} and owned by the operator, so the brief
window in which the bare entry is in place exposes only the
operator's own running garrison.

//axhos_step
. *Initialize workload's WSL via SSH-as-workload*: open an SSH
session as {bujb_workload_user} using {burp_workload_key_file}
through the just-placed bare authorized_keys entry. This session
constitutes a real Windows logon for the workload, with HKCU
mounted. Inside that session, perform in order: `wsl.exe --import
rbtww-main <workload-wsl-root> <seed-tarball-path>` to register
the workload's own rbtww-main in `HKCU\Lxss`, where
`<workload-wsl-root>` is a workload-writable directory inside the
workload's Windows profile by convention;
`wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root useradd
--create-home --shell /bin/bash ${BUJB_workload_user}` to mint
the inner Linux workload user; `passwd --lock` (root inside the
distribution) on that Linux user to enforce SSH-key-only on the
inner account; and plant {burp_workload_key_file}'s contents at
`/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519` (chmod 600) inside
the workload's distribution so the Linux session can use it for
outbound (e.g., GitHub). On exit, remove the seed tarball from
the admin side — the seed has served its purpose.

//axhos_step
. *Lock down workload trust*: rewrite the workload's authorized_keys
file with the locked-down `command=` form, replacing the bare entry
placed earlier. The rewrite happens from a workload SSH session
because the file is workload-owned (per the icacls grants in step 5)
and the workload's session has direct write privilege without
ownership gymnastics. The directive content is locked spec text,
mirrored at `BUJB_command_w` in {bujb_jurisdiction};
`${BUJB_workload_user}` is substituted with the canonical workload
user at directive-emit time:
+
[listing]
----
command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding
----

//axhos_step
. *Validate round-trip*: SSH as workload, run a no-op command,
verify exit 0. Validates the *locked-down* form: the workload
session is routed through `command=`, which dispatches to the
workload's own imported `rbtww-main` distribution and runs the
inner Linux user's shell.

//axhog_guarantee
On success the node has exactly one workload identity named
{bujb_workload_user} mirrored across all three namespaces: the
Windows local user (auth boundary, with profile registered in
`HKLM\...\ProfileList`), the workload-owned WSL distribution
`rbtww-main` (registered in workload's `HKCU\Lxss`), and the
WSL Linux user inside that distribution (execution context).

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds.
Non-zero on invigilate-windows assertion failure, admin SSH failure,
seed tarball export failure, account-management failure in any
namespace, `wsl --import` failure, key-write failure, or workload
validation failure.

content.text:
## Approach

BUSJGW at 203 lines is the largest of the BUSJ Windows family — narrative NOTE preamble (3 paragraphs ≈ 50 lines), 9 prose-shaped axhos_steps with multi-line bodies, monolithic axhog_guarantee paragraph, narrative axhoc_completion. The post-ABA target shape (per BUSJIW/CW/HW/GC):

- Big NOTE → 2-3 bounded short NOTEs (dense facts)
- Step bodies → 1-line sub-bullet facts where the fact admits, sparse `busc_*` voicing anchors at control points (unvoiced `..` lines for plain descriptive actions per RBSAC pattern)
- `{busc_fatal}` bodies → short em-dash cross-refs
- Drop explicit `{busc_use}` pairings (the «VAR» reference inside `{busc_require}` body is implicit use, matching RBSAV)
- Trailing qualifiers ride em-dash `--`
- axhog_guarantee → bullet list
- axhoc_completion → 2 sentences

«VAR» repertoire coordination across family: reuse `«ADMIN_SESSION»` (BUSJIW/GC), `«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` (BUSJCW/GC), `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` (BUSJGC). New for the 3-namespace WSL shape: `«WORKLOAD_SSH_SESSION»` (or `«WORKLOAD_LOGON»`), `«SEED_TARBALL»`, and ones I'll pick after reading the exemplars.

The step structure stays (9 steps, listing block for the `command=` directive preserved); only prose density changes. No bash edits.

**Execution: inline opus 4.7 — single file, full conversation context already carrying ABA's locked discipline.** Delegation buys nothing here (no parallelism).

**Plan:**
1. Read BUSJCW + BUSJGC + BUSJIW to lock the exemplar shape
2. Translate BUSJGW
3. Run gates: `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` + `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`
4. Report; you decide on wrap

Proceed?

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_caparison_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_caparison} on Windows -- three phases. Phase 1: admin SSH
5	key trust + `sshd_config` hardening. Phase 2: key-only reconnect.
6	Phase 3: post-trust admin posture (WSL distribution, powercfg,
7	Tailscale service). Closes with {bust_invigilate_windows}.
8	
9	NOTE: Nuclear -- every invocation purges and re-establishes the
10	caparison-managed posture; no skip-if-present logic.
11	{bust_handbook_windows} owns operator-manual posture; caparison owns
12	the idempotent admin-shell remainder.
13	
14	NOTE: Phase 1's `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session
15	-- the post-restart disconnect is the expected phase boundary, not a
16	failure. PowerShell-side operations use `Get-Content`, `Set-Service`,
17	`Start-Service`, `Restart-Service`, `wsl.exe --import`; bash-side
18	BCG-compliant matching extracts effective `sshd_config` directives
19	from the raw bytes.
20	
21	The {busn_caparison} operation on Windows:
22	
23	//axhopt_typed_parameter
24	{burp_investiture}
25	
26	//axhoq_precondition
27	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
28	{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node.
29	
30	//axhoq_precondition
31	{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
32	Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
33	applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
34	Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
35	auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
36	Caparison presupposes this scope.
37	
38	//axhoq_precondition
39	{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
40	{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH session*:
44	.. {busc_call}
45	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
46	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (steady-state: key via
47	{burp_privileged_key_file}; first run: TTY password)
48	.. {busc_store}
49	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` for phase 1 operations
50	.. {busc_fatal}
51	on connection failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Phase 1 — derive admin pubkey*:
55	.. {busc_call}
56	`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file}
57	.. {busc_store}
58	`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
59	.. {busc_fatal}
60	on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Phase 1 — install admin pubkey*:
64	.. write `«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` to `administrators_authorized_keys` over
65	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` as a bare key entry (no `command=`)
66	.. nuclear -- file replaced, not merged
67	.. {busc_fatal}
68	on write failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
72	.. {busc_call}
73	`icacls` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- ACL restricted to SYSTEM and
74	Administrators (Windows OpenSSH refuses the file otherwise);
75	idempotent
76	.. {busc_fatal}
77	on `icacls` failure -- phase 2 reconnect would fail
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Phase 1 — write hardened sshd_config directives*:
81	.. write `PubkeyAuthentication yes`, `PasswordAuthentication no`,
82	`PermitEmptyPasswords no` to `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`;
83	idempotent
84	.. {busc_fatal}
85	on write failure -- restart would still serve password auth
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Phase 1 — verify hardened directives took*:
89	.. {busc_call}
90	PowerShell `Get-Content` on `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`
91	.. {busc_store}
92	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»`
93	.. {busc_require}
94	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
95	`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
96	directive values (BCG-compliant matching)
97	.. {busc_fatal}
98	on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows
99	
100	//axhos_step
101	. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*:
102	.. {busc_call}
103	`sshd -t` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- penultimate atomic op of phase 1
104	.. {busc_store}
105	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
106	.. {busc_require}
107	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
108	.. {busc_fatal}
109	on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd
110	
111	//axhos_step
112	. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*:
113	.. {busc_call}
114	`Restart-Service sshd` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- final atomic op
115	of phase 1
116	.. {busc_fatal}
117	on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
118	expected phase boundary)
119	
120	//axhos_step
121	. *Phase 2 — reconnect under key-only auth*:
122	.. {busc_call}
123	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
124	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using {burp_privileged_key_file}
125	.. {busc_store}
126	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` for phase 3 operations
127	.. {busc_fatal}
128	on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
129	session); recover via operator's local console
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Phase 3 — stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
133	.. {busc_call}
134	`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over
135	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` to register `rbtww-main` under the admin user
136	(canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction})
137	.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
138	.. {busc_fatal}
139	on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
140	admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted
141	
142	//axhos_step
143	. *Phase 3 — disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
144	.. {busc_call}
145	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
146	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
147	`powercfg /hibernate off` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
148	.. {busc_fatal}
149	on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
150	Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)
151	
152	//axhos_step
153	. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
154	.. {busc_call}
155	`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
156	`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
157	.. {busc_fatal}
158	on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
159	{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)
160	
161	//axhos_step
162	. *Post-completion check — run invigilate-windows*:
163	.. {busc_call}
164	{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
165	.. {busc_store}
166	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
167	.. {busc_require}
168	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
169	.. {busc_fatal}
170	on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim
171	
172	//axhog_guarantee
173	On success:
174	
175	* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
176	  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
177	* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
178	  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`.
179	* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
180	* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user.
181	* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
182	* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
183	* {bust_invigilate_windows} confirms every posture fact end-to-end.
184	
185	//axhoc_completion
186	Exit 0 when the post-completion {bust_invigilate_windows} passes.
187	Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure (admin SSH, pubkey, ACL,
188	`sshd_config`, `sshd -t`), phase-2 reconnect failure, any phase-3 op
189	failure (`wsl --import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
190	invigilate failure.
191

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_cygwin}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell,
5	for Windows nodes reached via Cygwin sshd. Workload-only -- garrison
6	never touches `sshd_config` and never modifies admin authorized_keys.
7	
8	NOTE: Destruction is letter-agnostic via `zbujb_obliterate_workload`
9	in {bujb_jurisdiction} -- the single greppable destroy verb. Covenant
10	spans the Windows SAM entry, `C:\Users\<user>\` profile, the
11	`C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\` Cygwin home, and the WSL-side
12	`/home/<user>` inside `rbtww-main` when present. Each step idempotent
13	on absent state.
14	
15	NOTE: Admin trust is the responsibility of {busn_caparison} on the
16	Windows OpenSSH server; Cygwin sshd hardening (when run alongside
17	Windows OpenSSH) remains operator-manual.
18	
19	The {busn_garrison} operation on Windows via Cygwin:
20	
21	//axhopt_typed_parameter
22	{burp_investiture}
23	
24	//axhoq_precondition
25	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
26	{burn_regime} targets a Windows node with Cygwin sshd installed,
27	reachable as {burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth using
28	{burp_privileged_key_file}.
29	
30	//axhoq_precondition
31	{busn_caparison} has run for this {burp_investiture} -- admin SSH
32	trust established by Windows OpenSSH key-only auth.
33	
34	//axhoq_precondition
35	{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
36	station.
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Assert admin posture*:
40	.. {busc_call}
41	{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
42	.. {busc_store}
43	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
44	.. {busc_require}
45	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
46	.. {busc_fatal}
47	on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (operator-managed) or
48	{bust_caparison_windows} scope (caparison-managed); diagnostic
49	surfaced verbatim
50	
51	//axhos_step
52	. *Open admin SSH session*:
53	.. {busc_call}
54	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with {burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only
55	.. {busc_store}
56	`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
57	.. {busc_fatal}
58	on connection failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope
59	
60	//axhos_step
61	. *Obliterate prior workload*:
62	.. {busc_call}
63	`zbujb_obliterate_workload` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- single
64	PowerShell session purges the Windows SAM entry, profile directory,
65	Cygwin home, and (when `rbtww-main` is installed) the WSL-side Linux
66	user
67	.. nuclear -- idempotent on absent state
68	.. {busc_fatal}
69	on PowerShell session failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
70	
71	//axhos_step
72	. *Create workload account*:
73	.. provision {bujb_workload_user} as an unprivileged ssh-only Cygwin
74	account over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
75	.. {busc_fatal}
76	on Cygwin account-management failure -- prior obliterate left state
77	
78	//axhos_step
79	. *Place workload trust*:
80	.. {busc_call}
81	`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_workload_key_file}
82	.. {busc_store}
83	`«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` from stdout
84	.. write `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` to the workload's authorized_keys with
85	the shell-letter `c` `command=` directive (canonical:
86	`BUJB_command_c` in {bujb_jurisdiction}):
87	+
88	[listing]
89	----
90	command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding
91	----
92	.. {busc_fatal}
93	on key-write failure -- workload account left without trust
94	
95	//axhos_step
96	. *Plant workload key*:
97	.. copy {burp_workload_key_file} to the workload account at the
98	shell-letter `c` destination path (canonical: `BUJB_workload_keypath_c`
99	in {bujb_jurisdiction})
100	.. {busc_fatal}
101	on copy failure -- workload account reachable but key absent
102	
103	//axhos_step
104	. *Validate round-trip*:
105	.. {busc_call}
106	ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file}; run a
107	no-op command
108	.. {busc_store}
109	`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»`
110	.. {busc_require}
111	`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»` is zero
112	.. {busc_fatal}
113	on non-zero -- workload trust placed but unreachable
114	
115	//axhog_guarantee
116	On success:
117	
118	* Exactly one workload account named {bujb_workload_user} exists on
119	  the node, reachable via Cygwin bash.
120	* Workload authorized_keys carries `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` with the
121	  shell-letter `c` `command=` directive.
122	* {burp_workload_key_file} planted at the shell-letter `c` destination
123	  authenticates the workload round-trip.
124	* Admin posture is unchanged -- no `sshd_config` writes, no admin
125	  authorized_keys writes.
126	
127	//axhoc_completion
128	Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds. Non-zero on
129	invigilate-windows assertion failure, admin SSH failure, obliterate
130	failure, Cygwin account-management failure, key-write failure, or
131	workload round-trip failure.
132

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_invigilate_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_invigilate} on Windows -- read-only host posture
5	verification. Asserts each posture fact matches expected; no
6	mutations.
7	
8	NOTE: Invocation: {bust_caparison_windows} post-completion check;
9	{bust_garrison_cygwin} and {bust_garrison_wsl} precondition.
10	
11	NOTE: Failure pointers discriminate operator-managed
12	({bust_handbook_windows}) from caparison-managed
13	({bust_caparison_windows}). PowerShell reads via
14	`Get-ItemPropertyValue`, `Get-Service`, `powercfg /a`, `wsl.exe --list
15	--quiet`; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts each fact.
16	
17	The {busn_invigilate} operation on Windows:
18	
19	//axhopt_typed_parameter
20	{burp_investiture}
21	
22	//axhoq_precondition
23	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
24	{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node reachable as
25	{burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth using
26	{burp_privileged_key_file}. Admin SSH failure surfaces as the first
27	posture fact -- invigilate presupposes trust, does not establish it.
28	
29	//axhos_step
30	. *Open admin SSH session*:
31	.. {busc_call}
32	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with {burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only
33	.. {busc_store}
34	`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
35	.. {busc_fatal}
36	on connection failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Assert operator-managed registry keys*:
40	.. For each {bust_handbook_windows}-established registry value
41	(canonical: `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` under
42	`HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`):
43	... {busc_call}
44	PowerShell `Get-ItemPropertyValue` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
45	... {busc_store}
46	`«REG_VALUE»`
47	... {busc_require}
48	`«REG_VALUE»` matches expected
49	... {busc_fatal}
50	on mismatch -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
51	
52	//axhos_step
53	. *Assert AoAc override*:
54	.. {busc_call}
55	PowerShell `Get-ItemPropertyValue` for
56	`HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\PlatformAoAcOverride`
57	.. {busc_store}
58	`«REG_AOAC»`
59	.. {busc_require}
60	`«REG_AOAC»` equals `0`
61	.. {busc_fatal}
62	on mismatch -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern Standby override)
63	
64	//axhos_step
65	. *Assert Tailscale service registered*:
66	.. {busc_call}
67	PowerShell `Get-Service Tailscale`
68	.. {busc_store}
69	`«SVC_TAILSCALE»`
70	.. {busc_require}
71	`«SVC_TAILSCALE»` is non-empty
72	.. {busc_fatal}
73	on absence -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)
74	
75	//axhos_step
76	. *Assert Tailscale service StartupType*:
77	.. {busc_call}
78	PowerShell `Get-Service Tailscale | Select-Object StartType`
79	.. {busc_store}
80	`«SVC_STARTTYPE»`
81	.. {busc_require}
82	`«SVC_STARTTYPE»` equals `Automatic`
83	.. {busc_fatal}
84	on mismatch -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope
85	
86	//axhos_step
87	. *Assert powercfg sleep/hibernate disabled*:
88	.. {busc_call}
89	`powercfg /a` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
90	.. {busc_store}
91	`«POWERCFG_REPORT»`
92	.. {busc_require}
93	`«POWERCFG_REPORT»` reports neither standby nor hibernate as available
94	.. {busc_fatal}
95	on mismatch -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope (or AoAc override missing)
96	
97	//axhos_step
98	. *Assert admin's rbtww-main WSL distribution registered*:
99	.. {busc_call}
100	`wsl.exe --list --quiet` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
101	.. {busc_store}
102	`«WSL_DISTRO_LIST»`
103	.. {busc_require}
104	`«WSL_DISTRO_LIST»` contains `rbtww-main` (canonical:
105	`BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction})
106	.. {busc_fatal}
107	on absence -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope
108	
109	//axhog_guarantee
110	On success:
111	
112	* Every posture fact matches its expected value.
113	* `«ADMIN_SESSION»` was used only for read commands.
114	* No mutation occurred.
115	* The node is in the shape {bust_caparison_windows} produces and
116	  {bust_garrison_cygwin} / {bust_garrison_wsl} require.
117	
118	//axhoc_completion
119	Exit 0 when every posture fact matches expected. Non-zero on first
120	mismatch with diagnostic naming the failing fact, expected value,
121	observed value, and pointer ({bust_handbook_windows} for
122	operator-managed; {bust_caparison_windows} for caparison-managed).
123

content.text:
Now writing the translation. I have the locked discipline from BUSJCW (three-phase Windows shape, multi-call steps), BUSJGC (workload-only structure, listing-block pattern), and BUSJIW («VAR» repertoire baseline).

«VAR» repertoire for BUSJGW: `«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`, `«ADMIN_SESSION»`, `«SEED_TARBALL»`, `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»`, `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_INIT»`, `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_LOCKDOWN»`, `«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»` — two workload sessions structurally distinct (init vs lockdown), mirroring BUSJCW's `P1/P2` split.

input.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_garrison_wsl}

NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell, for
Windows nodes. Workload identity inhabits *three namespaces*: a Windows
local user (auth boundary discovered by Windows OpenSSH), the workload's
*own* registered WSL distribution `rbtww-main` (populated in workload's
`HKCU\Lxss` via `wsl --import` from a seed tarball), and a Linux user
inside that workload-owned distribution (execution context entered by
the locked-down `command=` directive's `wsl.exe --user`). The third
namespace is forced by Microsoft's per-user WSL design -- distributions
register only in the running user's `HKCU\Lxss`; admin's distributions
are invisible to workload sessions.

NOTE: Mechanism is *SSH-as-workload conducted by the privileged
orchestrator* -- bare workload authorized_keys → workload SSH session
imports `rbtww-main` into workload's `HKCU\Lxss` + mints inner Linux
user + plants privkey → locked-down `command=` rewrites the bare entry
from a second workload session. Two workload sessions are structurally
distinct -- first lands a real Windows logon (HKCU mounted); second
overwrites the workload-owned authorized_keys without ownership
gymnastics. The brief bare-authorized_keys window exposes only the
operator's own running garrison -- workload pubkey derived from
operator-owned {burp_workload_key_file}.

NOTE: Destruction is letter-agnostic via `zbujb_obliterate_workload` in
{bujb_jurisdiction} -- single greppable destroy verb covering the
Windows SAM entry, `C:\Users\<user>\` profile (which by convention
contains the workload's WSL filesystem root, so purging the profile
transitively destroys the workload's WSL distribution registration
on-disk and `HKCU\Lxss` hive), `C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\` Cygwin home,
and the WSL-side Linux user inside admin's `rbtww-main` (vestige from
prior garrison shapes; idempotent on absent state). Admin trust is
{busn_caparison}'s scope; sshd hardening inside the WSL distribution
remains operator-manual.

The {busn_garrison} operation on Windows via WSL:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node, reachable as
{burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth using
{burp_privileged_key_file}.

//axhoq_precondition
{busn_caparison} has run for this {burp_investiture} -- admin SSH trust
established, `rbtww-main` staged under the admin user (canonical:
`BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction}), operator-managed
registry posture / Tailscale / powercfg all in the shape
{bust_invigilate_windows} expects.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Assert admin posture*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
.. {busc_store}
`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (operator-managed) or
{bust_caparison_windows} scope (caparison-managed); diagnostic surfaced
verbatim

//axhos_step
. *Open admin SSH session*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with {burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
.. {busc_fatal}
on connection failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Export admin's rbtww-main as workload-readable seed*:
.. {busc_call}
PowerShell `wsl.exe --export rbtww-main <seed-path>` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
to a path inside the workload's Windows profile -- admin can write there
before the workload first logs in, workload session can read it
.. {busc_store}
`«SEED_TARBALL»` (the chosen path)
.. {busc_fatal}
on export failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope (rbtww-main missing
or admin HKCU\\Lxss corrupted)

//axhos_step
. *Obliterate prior workload*:
.. {busc_call}
`zbujb_obliterate_workload` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- single PowerShell
session purges Windows SAM entry, profile directory (transitively the
workload's WSL filesystem and `HKCU\Lxss` hive), Cygwin home, and the
WSL-side Linux user inside admin's `rbtww-main`
.. nuclear -- idempotent on absent state
.. {busc_fatal}
on PowerShell session failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Create Windows workload account*:
.. provision {bujb_workload_user} as a Windows local user via
`net.exe user /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
.. register the resulting SID under
`HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList` so
Windows OpenSSH builds a complete logon session on inbound SSH --
Win32-OpenSSH #1383
.. no Linux user is created inside admin's `rbtww-main` -- the
workload's Linux user is minted inside the workload's *own* imported
distribution in step 7
.. {busc_fatal}
on Windows account-management failure -- prior obliterate left state

//axhos_step
. *Place bare workload trust*:
.. {busc_call}
`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_workload_key_file}
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` from stdout
.. over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` create
`/mnt/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/` (mkdir -p, chmod 700) and
write `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` to `authorized_keys` (chmod 600) as a bare
entry -- locked-down `command=` would route through workload's
`HKCU\Lxss` before the import lands
.. NTFS ACLs inherited from the parent profile (admin-owned, non-admin
read-only) satisfy StrictModes -- POSIX `chown` meaningless to Windows
OpenSSH which inspects NTFS ACLs not POSIX modes
.. {busc_fatal}
on key-write failure -- workload account left without trust

//axhos_step
. *Initialize workload's WSL via SSH-as-workload*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file} through the
just-placed bare entry -- session is a real Windows logon for the
workload, HKCU mounted
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_SESSION_INIT»`
.. over `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_INIT»` run `wsl.exe --import rbtww-main
<workload-wsl-root> «SEED_TARBALL»` -- registers the workload's own
`rbtww-main` in workload's `HKCU\Lxss`; `<workload-wsl-root>` is a
workload-writable directory inside the workload's Windows profile
.. then `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root useradd
--create-home --shell /bin/bash ${BUJB_workload_user}` -- mints the
inner Linux workload user
.. then `passwd --lock ${BUJB_workload_user}` (root inside the
distribution) -- enforces SSH-key-only on the inner Linux account
.. then plant {burp_workload_key_file}'s contents at
`/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519` (chmod 600) inside the
distribution -- workload's outbound auth, e.g., GitHub
.. on session exit, delete `«SEED_TARBALL»` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` --
seed has served its purpose
.. {busc_fatal}
on workload session failure, `wsl --import` failure, inner `useradd` /
`passwd` failure, privkey plant failure, or seed delete failure --
workload state partially provisioned across namespaces

//axhos_step
. *Lock down workload trust*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file} through the
still-bare entry
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_SESSION_LOCKDOWN»`
.. over `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_LOCKDOWN»` overwrite the workload's
authorized_keys with the locked-down `command=` form, replacing the
bare entry -- workload-owned file, workload-driven write avoids
ownership-transfer gymnastics
.. canonical directive content: `BUJB_command_w` in {bujb_jurisdiction}
with `${BUJB_workload_user}` substituted at directive-emit time:
+
[listing]
----
command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding
----
.. {busc_fatal}
on workload session failure or rewrite failure -- bare trust still
in place; revalidate from a clean garrison

//axhos_step
. *Validate round-trip*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file}; run a no-op
command -- session routes through locked-down `command=`, dispatches
to workload's own imported `rbtww-main`, runs the inner Linux user's
shell
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- locked-down form broken; workload reachable bare but
not under the operational directive

//axhog_guarantee
On success:

* Exactly one workload identity named {bujb_workload_user} exists,
  mirrored across all three namespaces.
* Windows local user with SID registered under `HKLM\...\ProfileList`
  (auth boundary).
* Workload-owned `rbtww-main` registered in workload's `HKCU\Lxss`
  (distribution namespace).
* WSL Linux user inside that distribution (execution context entered
  by the locked-down `command=` directive's `wsl.exe --user`).
* Workload authorized_keys carries `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` with the
  shell-letter `w` `command=` directive.
* {burp_workload_key_file} planted at `/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519`
  inside the workload's distribution authenticates the round-trip.
* Admin posture is unchanged -- no `sshd_config` writes, no admin
  authorized_keys writes.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the locked-down round-trip validation succeeds. Non-zero on
invigilate-windows assertion failure, admin SSH failure, seed export
failure, obliterate failure, Windows account-management failure,

toolUseResult.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_garrison_wsl}

NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell, for
Windows nodes. Workload identity inhabits *three namespaces*: a Windows
local user (auth boundary discovered by Windows OpenSSH), the workload's
*own* registered WSL distribution `rbtww-main` (populated in workload's
`HKCU\Lxss` via `wsl --import` from a seed tarball), and a Linux user
inside that workload-owned distribution (execution context entered by
the locked-down `command=` directive's `wsl.exe --user`). The third
namespace is forced by Microsoft's per-user WSL design -- distributions
register only in the running user's `HKCU\Lxss`; admin's distributions
are invisible to workload sessions.

NOTE: Mechanism is *SSH-as-workload conducted by the privileged
orchestrator* -- bare workload authorized_keys → workload SSH session
imports `rbtww-main` into workload's `HKCU\Lxss` + mints inner Linux
user + plants privkey → locked-down `command=` rewrites the bare entry
from a second workload session. Two workload sessions are structurally
distinct -- first lands a real Windows logon (HKCU mounted); second
overwrites the workload-owned authorized_keys without ownership
gymnastics. The brief bare-authorized_keys window exposes only the
operator's own running garrison -- workload pubkey derived from
operator-owned {burp_workload_key_file}.

NOTE: Destruction is letter-agnostic via `zbujb_obliterate_workload` in
{bujb_jurisdiction} -- single greppable destroy verb covering the
Windows SAM entry, `C:\Users\<user>\` profile (which by convention
contains the workload's WSL filesystem root, so purging the profile
transitively destroys the workload's WSL distribution registration
on-disk and `HKCU\Lxss` hive), `C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\` Cygwin home,
and the WSL-side Linux user inside admin's `rbtww-main` (vestige from
prior garrison shapes; idempotent on absent state). Admin trust is
{busn_caparison}'s scope; sshd hardening inside the WSL distribution
remains operator-manual.

The {busn_garrison} operation on Windows via WSL:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node, reachable as
{burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth using
{burp_privileged_key_file}.

//axhoq_precondition
{busn_caparison} has run for this {burp_investiture} -- admin SSH trust
established, `rbtww-main` staged under the admin user (canonical:
`BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction}), operator-managed
registry posture / Tailscale / powercfg all in the shape
{bust_invigilate_windows} expects.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Assert admin posture*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
.. {busc_store}
`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (operator-managed) or
{bust_caparison_windows} scope (caparison-managed); diagnostic surfaced
verbatim

//axhos_step
. *Open admin SSH session*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with {burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
.. {busc_fatal}
on connection failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Export admin's rbtww-main as workload-readable seed*:
.. {busc_call}
PowerShell `wsl.exe --export rbtww-main <seed-path>` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
to a path inside the workload's Windows profile -- admin can write there
before the workload first logs in, workload session can read it
.. {busc_store}
`«SEED_TARBALL»` (the chosen path)
.. {busc_fatal}
on export failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope (rbtww-main missing
or admin HKCU\\Lxss corrupted)

//axhos_step
. *Obliterate prior workload*:
.. {busc_call}
`zbujb_obliterate_workload` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- single PowerShell
session purges Windows SAM entry, profile directory (transitively the
workload's WSL filesystem and `HKCU\Lxss` hive), Cygwin home, and the
WSL-side Linux user inside admin's `rbtww-main`
.. nuclear -- idempotent on absent state
.. {busc_fatal}
on PowerShell session failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Create Windows workload account*:
.. provision {bujb_workload_user} as a Windows local user via
`net.exe user /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
.. register the resulting SID under
`HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList` so
Windows OpenSSH builds a complete logon session on inbound SSH --
Win32-OpenSSH #1383
.. no Linux user is created inside admin's `rbtww-main` -- the
workload's Linux user is minted inside the workload's *own* imported
distribution in step 7
.. {busc_fatal}
on Windows account-management failure -- prior obliterate left state

//axhos_step
. *Place bare workload trust*:
.. {busc_call}
`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_workload_key_file}
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` from stdout
.. over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` create
`/mnt/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/` (mkdir -p, chmod 700) and
write `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` to `authorized_keys` (chmod 600) as a bare
entry -- locked-down `command=` would route through workload's
`HKCU\Lxss` before the import lands
.. NTFS ACLs inherited from the parent profile (admin-owned, non-admin
read-only) satisfy StrictModes -- POSIX `chown` meaningless to Windows
OpenSSH which inspects NTFS ACLs not POSIX modes
.. {busc_fatal}
on key-write failure -- workload account left without trust

//axhos_step
. *Initialize workload's WSL via SSH-as-workload*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file} through the
just-placed bare entry -- session is a real Windows logon for the
workload, HKCU mounted
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_SESSION_INIT»`
.. over `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_INIT»` run `wsl.exe --import rbtww-main
<workload-wsl-root> «SEED_TARBALL»` -- registers the workload's own
`rbtww-main` in workload's `HKCU\Lxss`; `<workload-wsl-root>` is a
workload-writable directory inside the workload's Windows profile
.. then `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root useradd
--create-home --shell /bin/bash ${BUJB_workload_user}` -- mints the
inner Linux workload user
.. then `passwd --lock ${BUJB_workload_user}` (root inside the
distribution) -- enforces SSH-key-only on the inner Linux account
.. then plant {burp_workload_key_file}'s contents at
`/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519` (chmod 600) inside the
distribution -- workload's outbound auth, e.g., GitHub
.. on session exit, delete `«SEED_TARBALL»` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` --
seed has served its purpose
.. {busc_fatal}
on workload session failure, `wsl --import` failure, inner `useradd` /
`passwd` failure, privkey plant failure, or seed delete failure --
workload state partially provisioned across namespaces

//axhos_step
. *Lock down workload trust*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file} through the
still-bare entry
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_SESSION_LOCKDOWN»`
.. over `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_LOCKDOWN»` overwrite the workload's
authorized_keys with the locked-down `command=` form, replacing the
bare entry -- workload-owned file, workload-driven write avoids
ownership-transfer gymnastics
.. canonical directive content: `BUJB_command_w` in {bujb_jurisdiction}
with `${BUJB_workload_user}` substituted at directive-emit time:
+
[listing]
----
command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding
----
.. {busc_fatal}
on workload session failure or rewrite failure -- bare trust still
in place; revalidate from a clean garrison

//axhos_step
. *Validate round-trip*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file}; run a no-op
command -- session routes through locked-down `command=`, dispatches
to workload's own imported `rbtww-main`, runs the inner Linux user's
shell
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- locked-down form broken; workload reachable bare but
not under the operational directive

//axhog_guarantee
On success:

* Exactly one workload identity named {bujb_workload_user} exists,
  mirrored across all three namespaces.
* Windows local user with SID registered under `HKLM\...\ProfileList`
  (auth boundary).
* Workload-owned `rbtww-main` registered in workload's `HKCU\Lxss`
  (distribution namespace).
* WSL Linux user inside that distribution (execution context entered
  by the locked-down `command=` directive's `wsl.exe --user`).
* Workload authorized_keys carries `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` with the
  shell-letter `w` `command=` directive.
* {burp_workload_key_file} planted at `/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519`
  inside the workload's distribution authenticates the round-trip.
* Admin posture is unchanged -- no `sshd_config` writes, no admin
  authorized_keys writes.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the locked-down round-trip validation succeeds. Non-zero on
invigilate-windows assertion failure, admin SSH failure, seed export
failure, obliterate failure, Windows account-management failure,
key-write failure, workload SSH session failure, `wsl --import` failure,
inner account-management failure, privkey plant failure, seed cleanup
failure, lockdown rewrite failure, or workload round-trip failure.

content.text:
Both gates green:
- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh`: 5 fixtures / 28 cases
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`: 4 fixtures / 98 cases (47 enrollment-validation + 27 regime-validation + 9 regime-smoke + 15 handbook-render — the last exercises all BUSJ* docs structurally)

## Summary

BUSJGW translated under post-ABA RBS prose discipline. 203 → 223 lines.

**Shape changes:**
- 5 narrative head paragraphs → 3 bounded NOTEs (scope+three-namespace / SSH-as-workload mechanism+two-session structure+bare-window argument / destruction covenant+admin-trust scope+inner-sshd scope)
- 4 preconditions → 3 consolidated (caparison-has-run line absorbs invigilate's posture-fact list, since step 1 IS the invigilate call)
- 9 axhos_steps preserved (matching original structural decomposition)
- Step bodies: sparse `busc_*` voicing anchors at control points; unvoiced `..` lines for plain descriptive actions per RBSAC pattern; trailing qualifiers ride em-dash `--`
- `{busc_fatal}` bodies compressed to short cross-refs (`-- {bust_caparison_windows} scope`, `-- {bust_handbook_windows} scope`)
- Listing block for the `command=` directive preserved with `+` continuation (BUSJGC step-5 pattern)
- Monolithic `axhog_guarantee` paragraph → 7-bullet list
- Narrative `axhoc_completion` → 2 sentences

**«VAR» repertoire:** `«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` (matches BUSJCW/GC), `«ADMIN_SESSION»` (matches BUSJIW/GC), `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` (matches BUSJGC), `«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»` (matches BUSJGC); new for the WSL shape: `«SEED_TARBALL»` (admin-side seed path), `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_INIT»` / `«WORKLOAD_SESSION_LOCKDOWN»` (two structurally distinct workload sessions, mirroring BUSJCW's `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` / `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` split).

**Sibling alignment:** BUSJGW now reads as a coherent sibling of BUSJIW (122), BUSJCW (190), BUSJHW (176), BUSJGC (131). 223 lines tracks BUSJGW's intrinsic step density — 9 steps with 3-namespace step 7 (6 inner sub-bullets) plus the listing block — comparable to BUSJHW's growth pattern from literal command listings.

content.text:
## Character
Design-locked; preflight module mint + uniform step-1 gate + garrison-b platform flavor branching. Larger pace — absorbed AAz's preflight-module scope because the preflight gate and the per-platform branching share the same step-1 rework.

## Docket

Locked decisions:

**Preflight module:**
- Mint `Tools/buk/bujp_preflight.sh` (acronym: BUJP) as the seat for all garrison preflights. Three platform entries (linux, mac, wsl) dispatched from garrison entry by `BURN_PLATFORM`.
- Migrate `zbujb_garrison_w_preflight` from bujb to bujp under the per-platform naming axis.
- BUJP entry added to acronym map in CLAUDE.md.
- BUJP entry in BUS0 mapping section.

**Garrison step 1 — uniform preflight gate** (replaces today's implicit "step 1 is the SSH connect itself"):
- Universal SSH-connectivity probe (key-only, BatchMode, no-op exec) for b/c/w. Failure names `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` and `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` and advises the platform's admin-trust establishment step.
- Elevation probe (`sudo -ln <command>` per-command, with the platform's expected command list derived from the existing `sudo -n` callsites in bujb_jurisdiction.sh) for b-linux, b-mac, and w-inside-WSL only. Garrison-c is exempt — fenestrate's Windows-admin key trust covers its elevation.
- Mac admin-group probe via `dseditgroup -o checkmember -m <user> admin`. On failure, diagnostic names the `dseditgroup -o edit` fix path.

**Sudoers posture:** operator-managed, like the existing `ssh-copy-id` prerequisite. Garrison verifies effective behavior (`sudo -ln` succeeds), not file contents. Recommended: `/etc/sudoers.d/` drop-in (mode 0440, root-owned, dot-free filename), reusing `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`. Scoped NOPASSWD recommended; blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations and called out as such.

**Failure recovery:** render sudoers snippet locally, scp to remote home-cache (not /tmp), validate with `visudo -cf` over ssh, then `buc_die` with copy-paste-safe `sudo install` line referencing the staged file. Probe before stage so the happy path makes no extra ssh round-trips. Elevation failure on the live `sudo -ln` probe emits the `visudo -f /etc/sudoers.d/bujb-garrison` invocation, the scoped NOPASSWD line with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` substituted, on the correct filesystem (WSL distro for w-inside-WSL, host for b-linux/b-mac).

**Garrison-b flavor branching:**
- Steps 2 (destroy) and 3 (create) fully branch on `BURN_PLATFORM` via per-platform flavor functions. Steps 4–5 differ only in home-dir prefix — promote to a kindle constant per platform. Step 6 identical across platforms.
  - Linux destroy/create: `userdel -r` / `useradd -m -s /bin/bash -N`.
  - Mac destroy/create: `sysadminctl -deleteUser` (default removes home) / `sysadminctl -addUser ... -shell /bin/bash`.
  - Workload shell forced to `/bin/bash` on every platform — macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path; bash is BUK.

**BUS0 spec updates:**
- Paragraph under §Remote Node Access naming preflight as the precondition layer.
- Garrison paragraph names linux/mac flavors of garrison-b as load-bearing structure (not implementation detail), names the elevation matrix across b/c/w platforms, and names the uniform step-1 preflight.
- BUJP entry in mapping section.

**Paddock Standing Notes added:**
- Mac Remote Login enabled (operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN target including self).
- Linux openssh-server installed and sshd enabled.
- Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` on the three sudo-affected garrison flavors (b-linux, b-mac, w-inside-WSL), and admin-group membership on mac, are operator-managed preconditions.
- Workload shell `/bin/bash` forced regardless of platform default.

Out of scope:
- `bujn-cerebro` (linux) and `bujn-rocket` (mac) BURN profile creation — operator-side, anytime.
- Practice paces exercising linux/mac garrison end-to-end — separate slate after this lands.
- Heat rename to `rbk-mvp-3-draft-jurisdiction-procedures` — separate `jjx_alter` call.

Done when:
- `bash -n` green on `bujp_preflight.sh`, `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`.
- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green.
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green at baseline (no regression).
- BUS0 garrison paragraph + §Remote Node Access preflight paragraph read coherently against the elevation matrix and uniform step-1 preflight.
- Preflight diagnostics name their respective fix paths (visudo -cf for sudo, dseditgroup -o edit for mac admin group); verifiable by inspection.
- BUJP entry added to acronym map in CLAUDE.md.
- Paddock Standing Notes landed.
- No grep finds of single-platform assumptions in garrison-b's user-management branches.

content.text:
# ₢A-AAy status — partial; centerpiece still outstanding

**Pace scope:** Mint `Tools/buk/bujp_preflight.sh` as the dedicated preflight module (BUJP), introduce a uniform step-1 preflight gate (SSH-connectivity + elevation + mac admin-group probes) for garrison b/c/w, and branch garrison-b's user-management steps on `BURN_PLATFORM` for linux/mac. Absorbed AAz's preflight-module scope because the gate and the platform branching share the same step-1 rework.

**Partially landed elsewhere** (probably AAy-adjacent paces I haven't traced):
- **Garrison-b platform flavor branching** for user destroy/create (steps 2/3) — `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` lines 437–443, 458, 628, 918, 941, 977: `case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in bubep_linux ... bubep_mac ...` with `userdel -r` (linux) vs `sysadminctl -deleteUser` / `sysadminctl -addUser` (mac) wired.
- **A w-specific preflight function** — `zbujb_garrison_w_preflight` (bujb_jurisdiction.sh:1184) verifies the WSL distribution presence; called from the garrison entry dispatcher at line 1399. **But** it lives in bujb, not the planned bujp module, and it's w-only.

**Still outstanding** (the docket's centerpiece):
- `bujp_preflight.sh` module mint with three platform entries (linux/mac/wsl) dispatched by `BURN_PLATFORM`.
- Migrate `zbujb_garrison_w_preflight` from bujb to bujp under the per-platform naming axis.
- **Uniform step-1 preflight gate** for b/c/w:
  - Universal SSH-connectivity probe (key-only, BatchMode, no-op exec) naming `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` / `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` on failure.
  - `sudo -ln <command>` elevation probe for b-linux, b-mac, w-inside-WSL — **not present** (no `sudo -ln` callsites in bujb).
  - Mac `dseditgroup -o checkmember -m <user> admin` probe — **not present**.
- **Sudoers failure-recovery flow** — render snippet locally, scp to remote home-cache, validate with `visudo -cf`, `buc_die` with copy-paste-safe `sudo install` line. **Not present** (no `visudo` callsites).
- **BUS0 spec updates** — preflight paragraph under §Remote Node Access; garrison paragraph naming linux/mac flavors + elevation matrix + uniform step-1 preflight; BUJP mapping-section entry. **Not landed** (grep finds no "preflight" / "elevation" / "sudoers" / "dseditgroup" in BUS0).
- **BUJP entry** in CLAUDE.md acronym map. **Not landed** (no BUJP in CLAUDE.md).
- **Paddock Standing Notes** — Mac Remote Login, Linux openssh-server + sshd enabled, sudo NOPASSWD on the three sudo-affected garrison flavors, mac admin-group membership, workload shell forced to `/bin/bash`. **Not landed** in the paddock standing notes.

**Recommendation:** Yes, the work largely still needs to be done. The garrison-b mac/linux user-management branching (a meaningful but smaller chunk) has been absorbed by sibling paces, but the AAz-merged preflight-module scope — which was the load-bearing reason this pace was chunky — is entirely outstanding. Worth considering: the docket may want a light reslate to acknowledge the already-landed garrison-b branching as a precondition (so its done-when doesn't re-claim that work), and the still-outstanding scope reads as preflight-module + uniform step-1 gate + sudoers recovery + BUS0/CLAUDE.md/paddock updates.

content.text:
# AAy is ready to start now — no hard blockers

**Concrete prerequisites checked, all in place:**

- `{bust_invigilate_macos}` / `{bust_invigilate_linux}` linked terms minted in BUS0 (BUS0:51–52, anchors at 1847/1854) — AAy's BUS0 prose updates can reference them.
- BUS0 §Remote Node Access section exists (BUS0:1640) — AAy's preflight paragraph location is ready.
- `BURN_PLATFORM` enum landed (`bubep_linux`/`bubep_mac`/`bubep_windows`) and garrison-b user-management already branches on it — AAy's preflight module dispatch by platform has its substrate.
- BUSJGB already has invigilate-{macos,linux} as precondition + first step (BUSJGB:30, 43–44) — AA6's "Align BUSJGB" done-when looks partially landed, separate from AAy's scope.

**Soft argument for ₢A-AA6 first (heat-order recommendation):**

The orient names ₢A-AA6 (caparison-invigilate-mac-linux-content) as the next actionable pace. AA6 fills the six non-Windows BUSJ stubs (BUSJCM/CL/IM/IL/HM/HL) natively against the now-complete five-file Windows exemplar set (BUSJIW + BUSJCW + BUSJHW + BUSJGW + BUSJGC). The mechanical-translation discipline is freshly loaded from this session's AA2 work; that cognitive mode is the right one for AA6's body-fill-against-exemplar work.

AAy's cognitive flavor is different — design conversation + bash module mint + bash code probe design + BUS0 architectural prose. Doing AA6 first preserves the translation-discipline focus; AAy after lets AAy reference a fully-settled BUSJ family.

**Soft argument for AAy first (defensible alternative):**

AAy's docket character notes "Larger pace — absorbed AAz's preflight-module scope." It's the chunkier, more design-loaded pace; landing it first puts the load-bearing preflight architecture in place before AA6's mac/linux BUSJ specs (which describe per-platform garrison ceremony) reference it implicitly. The BUS0 §Remote Node Access preflight paragraph and elevation-matrix prose would shape the architectural spine that the mac/linux BUSJ files sit within.

**Recommendation: AA6 next, AAy after.** Heat order is the orient's default for a reason, and the translation-discipline cognitive mode is well-loaded right now — letting AA6 ride that mode is cheaper than the context-switch into AAy's design + code-mint flavor. Either ordering is defensible; this is operator preference, not a dependency constraint.

content.text:
## Character
Body-fill plus precondition alignment for the six non-Windows BUSJ sub-spec stubs, authored natively against the post-AA_ exemplar (BUSJIW). Reads as ABA's mac/linux sibling pace — the same mechanical-translation rigor, but writing into empty stubs rather than translating existing prose. Land only after ABA so the exemplar set (BUSJIW + the three ABA-translated Windows files) is complete and the new style is a settled target.

## Goal
Fill body content of BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL natively against the post-AA_ Control Voicings vocabulary (`busc_*`, `bush_*`) and the BUSJIW exemplar's structural shape (sub-step grammar with control verbs, `«VAR»` cross-state names, bounded NOTEs, decomposed `axhog_guarantee`). Align BUSJGB's precondition list to reference `{bust_invigilate_macos}` / `{bust_invigilate_linux}` and add an Assert-admin-posture first step mirroring BUSJGW's.

## Boundary
- Files in scope: BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL, BUSJGB.
- BUSJCM/CL and BUSJIM/IL adopt `busc_*` (bash console verbs); BUSJHM/HL adopt `bush_*` (handbook verbs); mirror BUSJIW's tag distribution.
- `«VAR»` names align with BUSJIW where shared (e.g., `«ADMIN_SESSION»`); introduce per-platform names where they don't cross over.
- Caparison-{macos,linux} ceremony shape: admin SSH key-auth precondition (ssh-copy-id is operator-managed per handbook), then idempotent host posture (Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale launchd on Mac; sshd + sleep-target mask + tailscaled on Linux), closing with invigilate-{platform}.
- Invigilate-{macos,linux}: posture fact lists with read commands + expected values; failure pointers to handbook (operator-managed) vs caparison (caparison-managed).
- Handbook-{macos,linux}: operator-manual scope per platform — Tailscale install + login, ssh-copy-id first-run setup, sudoers NOPASSWD entries, anything genuinely operator-side.
- BUSJGB: precondition list references invigilate-{macos,linux}; first step asserts admin posture via invigilate; mirror BUSJGW's pattern.
- No BUS0 edits — vocabulary already minted in AA_.
- No bash code edits.

## Done when
- All six non-Windows BUSJ files read as siblings of BUSJIW and ABA-translated BUSJCW/HW/GC.
- BUSJGB precondition list and first step reference invigilate-{macos,linux}.
- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green.

9	{bust_garrison_bash} precondition on macOS nodes.
10	
11	NOTE: Failure pointers discriminate operator-managed
12	({bust_handbook_macos}) from caparison-managed
13	({bust_caparison_macos}). Reads via `systemsetup -getremotelogin`,
14	`pmset -g`, `launchctl list`; bash-side BCG-compliant matching
15	extracts each fact.

17	The {busn_invigilate} operation on macOS:
18	
19	//axhopt_typed_parameter
20	{burp_investiture}
21	
22	//axhoq_precondition
23	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
24	{burn_regime} targets a macOS node reachable as
25	{burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth using

30	. *Open admin SSH session*:
31	.. {busc_call}
32	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with {burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only
33	.. {busc_store}

42	.. {busc_store}
43	`«REMOTE_LOGIN_STATE»`
44	.. {busc_require}
45	`«REMOTE_LOGIN_STATE»` reports `On`
46	.. {busc_fatal}
47	on mismatch -- {bust_caparison_macos} scope
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Assert pmset sleep, displaysleep, and hibernate disabled*:
51	.. {busc_call}
52	`pmset -g` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
53	.. {busc_store}
54	`«PMSET_REPORT»`
55	.. {busc_require}
56	`«PMSET_REPORT»` parses to `sleep 0`, `displaysleep 0`, and
57	`hibernatemode 0` (BCG-compliant matching)
58	.. {busc_fatal}
59	on mismatch -- {bust_caparison_macos} scope
60

BUSJIM (97 lines, 5 facts, voicing 5/5/4/5) + BUSJIL (111 lines, 6 facts, voicing 6/6/5/6) with three NOTEs each mirroring BUSJIW. Posture-fact discrimination: BUSJIM split Tailscale into registered (operator pointer) + loaded-and-running (caparison pointer) mirroring BUSJIW's Tailscale-registered/StartupType pair; BUSJIL split sshd and tailscaled into enabled+active pairs (4 systemctl checks total). BUSJIL fatals on enabled facts carry dual-clause pointers (`-- {bust_handbook_linux} scope (openssh-server install) on unit-not-found; otherwise {bust_caparison_linux} scope`) -- longer than ABA's clipped pattern but preserves load-bearing operator-vs-caparison discrimination per BUSJIW's parenthetical-clarifier convention. BUSJHM (114 lines) + BUSJHL (132 lines): 6 operator-manual steps each, voicing 5+5+1 / 7+6+1 across bush_show/require/warn; bush_warn appears once per file at the sudoers-edit step (visudo lockout caution); literal commands in [listing] ---- blocks via + continuation per BUSJHW. BUSJGB alignment: added invigilate-passes precondition referencing {bust_invigilate_macos}/{bust_invigilate_linux} (selected by BURN-targeted OS), added Assert-admin-posture first step mirroring BUSJGW's pattern (failure surfaces fact-named diagnostic with {bust_handbook_{macos,linux}}/{bust_caparison_{macos,linux}} pointer dispatch), updated axhoc_completion to include invigilate-assertion-failure in non-zero conditions. No BUS0 vocabulary edits, no bash code edits, no edits to BUSJ files outside the seven in scope. Gates green: tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh 5 fixtures / 28 cases; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 4 fixtures / 98 cases (47 enrollment-validation + 27 regime-validation + 9 regime-smoke + 15 handbook-render). The handbook-render fixture exercises all BUSJ* docs, so green here covers the AsciiDoc structural correctness of all seven modified files under their new shape. Heat returns to: all 10 BUSJ sub-specs (CM/CL/CW, IM/IL/IW, HM/HL/HW, GB/GC) sit as a coherent RBS-disciplined family targeting jurisdiction's Caparison/Invigilate/Handbook/Garrison verbs across all three platforms; BUSJGW remains out of scope (later garrison-w redraft pace).

6940c1b0 jjb:1011-097d740b:₢A-AA6:n: Fill body content of BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM modeled on BUSJCW/BUSJIW prose shape (NOTE preamble, verb-mood steps, long guarantee). Caparison-{macos,linux} adopt single-phase ceremony wrapping operator-manual ssh-copy-id: admin SSH key-auth precondition, then idempotent reinforce of host posture (Remote Login + pmset on Mac; sshd + sleep-target mask + tailscaled on Linux), closing with invigilate-{platform}. BUSJIM lists four posture facts (admin SSH, Remote Login on, pmset values, Tailscale daemon registered+running) with operator-handbook vs caparison failure pointers.
